cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what the Newsweek reviewer thinks of the movie based on the passage. First, I need to look at the relevant parts of the text. The passage mentions: "Not all the reviews of the movie have been full of praise. A reviewer for Newsweek, who was given an early preview, acknowledged that the 40-minute sequence showing the attack itself was powerful. The attack comes quite late in the movie, however, and the reviewer was less impressed with the characters and the love story. 'Almost every line of the dialogue sounds like it comes from an old movie,' the reviewer wrote." So the reviewer thought the attack scenes were powerful, but the characters and love story were less impressive. Also, the dialogue is criticized for sounding like old movies, implying it's not original. Looking at the options: A) The scenes of the attack leave no impression. But the reviewer said they were powerful, so A is wrong. B) Dialogue is original and creative. The reviewer says the opposite, that it sounds like old movies, so B is incorrect. C) Characters and love story are less attractive. This matches the part where the reviewer was less impressed with them. So C seems correct. D) Attack scenes occupy too much time.
